records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.2189 - "The pilgrim, when they stand at Al-Mash'ar (Muzdalifah), is cleansed of their sins."
records.arabicText: 2189 - وَ اَلْحَاجُّ إِذَا وَقَفَ بِالْمَشْعَرِ خَرَجَ مِنْ ذُنُوبِهِ ».

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2345 - Sulaiman ibn Khalid asked him (Abu Abdullah (as)) about a man who cuts from the arak tree in Mecca.
Imam (as) replied: "He must pay its value as charity, and no one should remove anything from the trees of Mecca except for palm trees and fruit trees."
records.arabicText: 2345 - وَ سَأَلَهُ سُلَيْمَانُ بْنُ خَالِدٍ : عَنِ اَلرَّجُلِ يَقْطَعُ مِنَ اَلْأَرَاكِ اَلَّذِي بِمَكَّةَ قَالَ «عَلَيْهِ ثَمَنُهُ يَتَصَدَّقُ بِهِ وَ لاَ يَنْزِعُ مِنْ شَجَرِ مَكَّةَ شَيْئاً إِلاَّ اَلنَّخْلَ وَ شَجَرَ اَلْفَوَاكِهِ».
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.2345 - Sulaiman ibn Khalid asked him (Abu Abdullah (as)) about a man who cuts from the arak tree in Mecca.
Imam (as) replied:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "He must pay its value as charity, and no one should remove anything from the trees of Mecca except for palm trees and fruit trees."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2346 - It has been narrated from Muhammad ibn Muslim, from one of the Imams (as), who said:
I asked him: "Can a person in the state of ihram remove grass from outside the Haram?"
Imam (as) replied: "Yes."
I then asked: "What about from within the Haram?"
Imam (as) replied: "No."
records.arabicText: 2346 - وَ رَوَى مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ مُسْلِمٍ عَنْ أَحَدِهِمَا عَلَيْهِمَا اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : قُلْتُ لَهُ اَلْمُحْرِمُ يَنْزِعُ اَلْحَشِيشَ مِنْ غَيْرِ اَلْحَرَمِ فَقَالَ «نَعَمْ» قُلْتُ فَمِنَ اَلْحَرَمِ قَالَ «لاَ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2346 - It has been narrated from Muhammad ibn Muslim, from one of the Imams (as), who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
I asked him: "Can a person in the state of ihram remove grass from outside the Haram?"
Imam (as) replied: "Yes."
I then asked: "What about from within the Haram?"
Imam (as) replied: "No."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.2190 - The Messenger of Allah (swt), peace and blessings be upon him and his family, said: "Choose the best of your sacrifices, for they are your mounts on the Sirat (Bridge)."
records.arabicText: 2190 - وَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ : «اِسْتَفْرِهُوا ضَحَايَاكُمْ فَإِنَّهَا مَطَايَاكُمْ عَلَى اَلصِّرَاطِ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2190 - The Messenger of Allah (swt), peace and blessings be upon him and his family, said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Choose the best of your sacrifices, for they are your mounts on the Sirat (Bridge)."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2347 - Ishaq ibn Yazid asked Abu Ja'far (as) about a man entering Mecca and cutting from its trees.
Imam (as) replied: "Cut what encroaches into your space, but do not cut what does not enter into your dwelling."
records.arabicText: 2347 - وَ سَأَلَ إِسْحَاقُ بْنُ يَزِيدَ أَبَا جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : عَنِ اَلرَّجُلِ يَدْخُلُ مَكَّةَ فَيَقْطَعُ مِنْ شَجَرِهَا فَقَالَ «اِقْطَعْ مَا كَانَ دَاخِلاً عَلَيْكَ وَ لاَ تَقْطَعْ مَا لَمْ يَدْخُلْ مَنْزِلَكَ عَلَيْكَ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.2347 - Ishaq ibn Yazid asked Abu Ja'far (as) about a man entering Mecca and cutting from its trees.
Imam (as) replied:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Cut what encroaches into your space, but do not cut what does not enter into your dwelling."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2191 - Umm Salamah, may Allah (swt) be pleased with her, came to the Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him and his family, and said:
"O Messenger of Allah (swt), the Day of Sacrifice is approaching, and I do not have the means to afford a sacrifice. Should I borrow and offer a sacrifice?"
He (sw) replied: "Borrow and offer a sacrifice, for it is a debt that will be repaid."
records.arabicText: 2191 - وَ جَاءَتْ أُمُّ سَلَمَةَ رَضِيَ اَللَّهُ عَنْهَا إِلَى اَلنَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ فَقَالَتْ يَا رَسُولَ اَللَّهِ يَحْضُرُ اَلْأَضْحَى وَ لَيْسَ عِنْدِي ثَمَنُ اَلْأُضْحِيَّةِ فَأَسْتَقْرِضُ وَ أُضَحِّي فَقَالَ «اِسْتَقْرِضِي وَ ضَحِّي فَإِنَّهُ دَيْنٌ مَقْضِيٌّ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2191 - Umm Salamah, may Allah (swt) be pleased with her, came to the Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him and his family, and said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"O Messenger of Allah (swt), the Day of Sacrifice is approaching, and I do not have the means to afford a sacrifice. Should I borrow and offer a sacrifice?"
He (sw) replied: "Borrow and offer a sacrifice, for it is a debt that will be repaid."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2348 - Mansour bin Hazim asked Abu Abdullah (as) about the arak tree being in the sanctuary, and if he could cut it down.
Imam (as) said: "You may do so, and may you be ransomed for it."
records.arabicText: 2348 - وَ سَأَلَ مَنْصُورُ بْنُ حَازِمٍ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : عَنِ اَلْأَرَاكِ يَكُونُ فِي اَلْحَرَمِ فَأَقْطَعُهُ قَالَ «عَلَيْكَ فِدَاؤُهُ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2348 - Mansour bin Hazim asked Abu Abdullah (as) about the arak tree being in the sanctuary, and if he could cut it down.
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) said: "You may do so, and may you be ransomed for it."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.2192 - And the owner of the sacrifice is forgiven at the very first drop of its blood that falls.
records.arabicText: 2192 - وَ يُغْفَرُ لِصَاحِبِ اَلْأُضْحِيَّةِ عِنْدَ أَوَّلِ قَطْرَةٍ تَقْطُرُ مِنْ دَمِهَا ».
records.thaqalaynMatn: the owner of the sacrifice is forgiven at the very first drop of its blood that falls.

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2349 - It has been narrated from Ibrahim ibn Umar, from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
"There are two types of lost property (luqata). As for the lost property of the Haram, you must announce it for one year. If you find its owner, return it; otherwise, give it in charity. As for lost property outside the Haram, announce it for one year. If its owner comes, return it; otherwise, it becomes like your own property."
It has also been narrated:
"Among the names of Mecca are: Mecca, Bakkah, Umm al-Qura, Umm al-Rahm, and Al-Bassah. When people commit oppression there, it destroys them (bassathum, meaning annihilates them), and when they are oppressed there, they are shown mercy."
records.arabicText:
2349 - وَ رَوَى إِبْرَاهِيمُ بْنُ عُمَرَ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : «اَللُّقَطَةُ لُقَطَتَانِ لُقَطَةُ اَلْحَرَمِ تُعَرِّفُ سَنَةً فَإِنْ وَجَدْتَ صَاحِبَهَا وَ إِلاَّ تَصَدَّقْتَ بِهَا وَ لُقَطَةُ غَيْرِ اَلْحَرَمِ تُعَرِّفُهَا سَنَةً فَإِنْ جَاءَ صَاحِبُهَا وَ إِلاَّ فَهِيَ كَسَبِيلِ مَالِكَ ».
وَ رُوِيَ: «أَنَّ فِي أَسْمَاءِ مَكَّةَ أَنَّهَا مَكَّةُ وَ بَكَّةُ وَ أُمُّ اَلْقُرَى وَ أُمُّ رُحْمٍ وَ اَلْبَاسَّةُ كَانُوا إِذَا ظَلَمُوا بِهَا بَسَّتْهُمْ أَيْ أَهْلَكَتْهُمْ وَ كَانُوا إِذَا ظُلِمُوا رُحِمُوا ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2349 - It has been narrated from Ibrahim ibn Umar, from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"There are two types of lost property (luqata). As for the lost property of the Haram, you must announce it for one year. If you find its owner, return it; otherwise, give it in charity. As for lost property outside the Haram, announce it for one year. If its owner comes, return it; otherwise, it becomes like your own property."
It has also been narrated:
"Among the names of Mecca are: Mecca, Bakkah, Umm al-Qura, Umm al-Rahm, and Al-Bassah. When people commit oppression there, it destroys them (bassathum, meaning annihilates them), and when they are oppressed there, they are shown mercy."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.2193 - Abu Ja’far (as) said: "The practice of marking the sacrificial animal (Ish’ar) was deemed good because at the very first drop of its blood that falls, Allah (swt) forgives the person for that act."
records.arabicText: 2193 - وَ قَالَ أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : «إِنَّمَا اِسْتَحْسَنُوا إِشْعَارَ اَلْبُدْنِ لِأَنَّ أَوَّلَ قَطْرَةٍ تَقْطُرُ مِنْ دَمِهَا يَغْفِرُ اَللَّهُ لَهُ عَلَى ذَلِكَ ».
records.thaqalaynMatn: "The practice of marking the sacrificial animal (Ish’ar) was deemed good because at the very first drop of its blood that falls, Allah (swt) forgives the person for that act."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.2194 - "Whoever restrains their eyes, tongue, and hands during the Days of Tashreeq, Allah (swt), Mighty and Majestic, will record for them the reward of a Hajj in the following year."
records.arabicText: 2194 - وَ مَنْ كَفَّ بَصَرَهُ وَ لِسَانَهُ وَ يَدَهُ، أَيَّامَ اَلتَّشْرِيقِ كَتَبَ اَللَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ لَهُ مِثْلَ حَجٍّ مِنْ قَابِلٍ ».

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.2195 - The Messenger of Allah (swt), peace and blessings be upon him and his family, said: "Casting the pebbles at the Jamarat is a provision for the Day of Judgment."
records.arabicText: 2195 - وَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ : «رَمْيُ اَلْجِمَارِ ذُخْرٌ يَوْمَ اَلْقِيَامَةِ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2195 - The Messenger of Allah (swt), peace and blessings be upon him and his family, said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Casting the pebbles at the Jamarat is a provision for the Day of Judgment."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.2196 - The Messenger of Allah (swt), peace and blessings be upon him and his family, said: "When the pilgrim casts the pebbles at the Jamarat, they are cleansed of their sins."
records.arabicText: 2196 - وَ قَالَ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ : «اَلْحَاجُّ إِذَا رَمَى اَلْجِمَارَ خَرَجَ مِنْ ذُنُوبِهِ.

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2197 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
"Whoever casts the pebbles at the Jamarat will have a major destructive sin removed for each pebble. When a believer casts it, the angel catches it. But when a disbeliever casts it, Satan says mockingly, 'You did not truly strike me.'"
records.arabicText: 2197 - وَ قَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : «مَنْ رَمَى اَلْجِمَارَ يُحَطُّ عَنْهُ بِكُلِّ حَصَاةٍ كَبِيرَةٌ مُوبِقَةٌ وَ إِذَا رَمَاهَا اَلْمُؤْمِنُ اِلْتَقَفَهَا اَلْمَلَكُ وَ إِذَا رَمَاهَا اَلْكَافِرُ قَالَ اَلشَّيْطَانُ بِاسْتِكَ مَا رَمَيْتَ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2197 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Whoever casts the pebbles at the Jamarat will have a major destructive sin removed for each pebble. When a believer casts it, the angel catches it. But when a disbeliever casts it, Satan says mockingly, 'You did not truly strike me.'"

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.2198 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said: "When a believer shaves their head at Mina and buries the hair, they will come on the Day of Judgment, and every hair will have a fluent tongue calling out the Talbiyah in the name of its owner."
records.arabicText: 2198 - وَ قَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : «إِنَّ اَلْمُؤْمِنَ إِذَا حَلَقَ رَأْسَهُ بِمِنًى ثُمَّ دَفَنَهُ جَاءَ يَوْمَ اَلْقِيَامَةِ وَ كُلُّ شَعْرَةٍ لَهَا لِسَانٌ طَلْقٌ تُلَبِّي بِاسْمِ صَاحِبِهَا».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2198 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "When a believer shaves their head at Mina and buries the hair, they will come on the Day of Judgment, and every hair will have a fluent tongue calling out the Talbiyah in the name of its owner."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.2199 - The Messenger of Allah (swt), peace and blessings be upon him and his family, sought forgiveness for those who shaved their heads three times and for those who trimmed their hair once.
records.arabicText: 2199 - وَ اِسْتَغْفَرَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ لِلْمُحَلِّقِينَ ثَلاَثَ مَرَّاتٍ وَ لِلْمُقَصِّرِينَ مَرَّةً.
records.thaqalaynMatn: The Messenger of Allah (swt), peace and blessings be upon him and his family, sought forgiveness for those who shaved their heads three times and for those who trimmed their hair once.

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.2200 - It has been narrated: "Whoever shaves their head at Mina will have a light for every hair on the Day of Judgment."
records.arabicText: 2200 - وَ رُوِيَ : «أَنَّ مَنْ حَلَقَ رَأْسَهُ بِمِنًى كَانَ لَهُ بِكُلِّ شَعْرَةٍ نُورٌ يَوْمَ اَلْقِيَامَةِ ».

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2201 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) was asked about the words of Allah (swt), Mighty and Majestic: "Then whoever hastens to leave in two days, there is no sin upon him; and whoever delays, there is no sin upon him" (Surah Al-Baqarah, 2:203).
Imam (as) said: "They return forgiven, with no sin upon them."
records.arabicText: 2201 - وَ سُئِلَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : عَنْ قَوْلِ اَللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ «۝ فَمَنْ تَعَجَّلَ فِي يَوْمَيْنِ فَلاٰ إِثْمَ عَلَيْهِ وَ مَنْ تَأَخَّرَ فَلاٰ إِثْمَ عَلَيْهِ ۝» قَالَ «يَرْجِعُ مَغْفُوراً لاَ ذَنْبَ لَهُ».
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.2201 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) was asked about the words of Allah (swt), Mighty and Majestic: "Then whoever hastens to leave in two days, there is no sin upon him; and whoever delays, there is no sin upon him" (Surah Al-Baqarah, 2:203).
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "They return forgiven, with no sin upon them."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.2202 - It has been narrated: "They emerge from their sins as pure as the day their mother gave birth to them."
records.arabicText: 2202 - وَ رُوِيَ : «يَخْرُجُ مِنْ ذُنُوبِهِ كَنَحْوِ مَا وَلَدَتْهُ أُمُّهُ ».

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.2203 - Imam (as) said: "A servant remains within the sanctity of those performing Tawaf around the Kaaba as long as the hair from their shaving remains upon them."
records.arabicText: 2203 - وَ قَالَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : «لاَ يَزَالُ اَلْعَبْدُ فِي حَدِّ اَلطَّائِفِ بِالْكَعْبَةِ مَا دَامَ شَعْرُ اَلْحَلْقِ عَلَيْهِ ».
records.thaqalaynMatn: "A servant remains within the sanctity of those performing Tawaf around the Kaaba as long as the hair from their shaving remains upon them."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.2204 - It has been narrated: "The pilgrim, from the moment they leave their home until they return, is in the status of one performing Tawaf around the Kaaba."
records.arabicText: 2204 - وَ رُوِيَ : «أَنَّ اَلْحَاجَّ مِنْ حِينِ يَخْرُجُ مِنْ مَنْزِلِهِ حَتَّى يَرْجِعَ بِمَنْزِلَةِ اَلطَّائِفِ بِالْكَعْبَةِ ».
records.thaqalaynMatn: "The pilgrim, from the moment they leave their home until they return, is in the status of one performing Tawaf around the Kaaba."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.2205 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said: "Whoever performs the Hajj of Islam has untied a knot of the Fire from around their neck. Whoever performs two Hajj will remain in goodness until they die. And whoever performs three consecutive Hajj, whether they perform another Hajj afterward or not, is regarded as one devoted to Hajj."
records.arabicText: 2205 - وَ قَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : «مَنْ حَجَّ حَجَّةَ اَلْإِسْلاَمِ فَقَدْ حَلَّ عُقْدَةً مِنَ اَلنَّارِ مِنْ عُنُقِهِ وَ مَنْ حَجَّ حَجَّتَيْنِ لَمْ يَزَلْ فِي خَيْرٍ حَتَّى يَمُوتَ وَ مَنْ حَجَّ ثَلاَثَ حِجَجٍ مُتَوَالِيَةٍ ثُمَّ حَجَّ أَوْ لَمْ يَحُجَّ فَهُوَ بِمَنْزِلَةِ مُدْمِنِ اَلْحَجِّ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2205 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Whoever performs the Hajj of Islam has untied a knot of the Fire from around their neck. Whoever performs two Hajj will remain in goodness until they die. And whoever performs three consecutive Hajj, whether they perform another Hajj afterward or not, is regarded as one devoted to Hajj."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.2206 - It has been narrated: "Whoever performs three Hajj will never experience poverty."
records.arabicText: 2206 - وَ رُوِيَ : «أَنَّ مَنْ حَجَّ ثَلاَثَ حِجَجٍ لَمْ يُصِبْهُ فَقْرٌ أَبَداً».
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Whoever performs three Hajj will never experience poverty."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2207 - "Any camel on which Hajj is performed for three years will be made among the livestock of Paradise."
It has also been narrated: "Seven years."
records.arabicText:
2207 - وَ أَيُّمَا بَعِيرٍ حُجَّ عَلَيْهِ ثَلاَثَ سِنِينَ جُعِلَ مِنْ نَعَمِ اَلْجَنَّةِ ».
وروي " سبع سنين ".

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.2208 - Imam Ali ibn Musa Ar-Ridha (as) said: "Whoever facilitates Hajj for three believers has purchased their soul from Allah (swt), Mighty and Majestic, at its price, and Allah (swt) will not question them about how they earned their wealth, whether it was lawful or unlawful."
records.arabicText: 2208 - وَ قَالَ اَلرِّضَا عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : «مَنْ حَجَّ بِثَلاَثَةٍ مِنَ اَلْمُؤْمِنِينَ فَقَدِ اِشْتَرَى نَفْسَهُ مِنَ اَللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ بِالثَّمَنِ وَ لَمْ يَسْأَلْهُ مِنْ أَيْنَ اِكْتَسَبَ مَالَهُ مِنْ حَلاَلٍ أَوْ حَرَامٍ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2208 - Imam Ali ibn Musa Ar-Ridha (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Whoever facilitates Hajj for three believers has purchased their soul from Allah (swt), Mighty and Majestic, at its price, and Allah (swt) will not question them about how they earned their wealth, whether it was lawful or unlawful."
